title: neonauclea lanceolata
intro: Neonauclea lanceolata, also known as the lance-leaved Neonauclea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. Neonauclea lanceolata is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Philippines, and has been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and reduce pain. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including the potential to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of heart disease, as well as its possible antiseptic and antiviral properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Neonauclea lanceolata is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical applications, although further research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties and potential health benefits.
